etymology.description: phonetic 网 (mǎng) + semantic 山 (shān, "mountain") -> ridge
etymology.interpretation: The character combines the semantic 山 (shān, "mountain") suggesting a topographical feature, with the phonetic 网 (mǎng) to provide the sound. A ridge is a prominent mountain feature, making this combination intuitive for indicating 'ridge'.

examples.chinese: 那座高冈(那座高岡)，是徒步旅行的好地方(是徒步旅行的好地方)。
examples.pinyin: Nà zuò gāo gāng, shì túbù lǚxíng de hǎo dìfāng.
examples.english: That high ridge is a great place for hiking.

examples.chinese: 太阳从山冈升起(太陽從山岡升起)，洒下金色的光芒(灑下金色的光芒)。
examples.pinyin: Tàiyáng cóng shān gāng shēng qǐ, sǎ xià jīnsè de guāngmáng.
examples.english: The sun rises from the ridge, casting golden rays.

examples.chinese: 在冈顶上(在岡頂上)，你可以看到整个村庄(你可以看到整個村莊)。
examples.pinyin: Zài gāng dǐng shàng, nǐ kěyǐ kàn dào zhěnggè cūnzhuāng.
examples.english: On the top of the ridge, you can see the entire village.
